project.name: panda
project.description: panda OBD-II adapter firmware and Python drivers. Safety-critical CAN/CAN FD communication interface for openpilot vehicle control.

layers.name: Firmware Core (STM32)
layers.description: Low-level STM32H7 hardware abstraction, board initialization, and core firmware drivers.

layers.name: Safety Layer
layers.description: Safety-critical logic and verification for vehicle control CAN messages. Enforces safety models from opendbc.

layers.name: CAN Bus Drivers
layers.description: CAN/CAN FD drivers and hardware communication layer for STM32.

layers.name: Python Userspace Interface
layers.description: Python library for host-side communication with panda. Provides userspace API for CAN operations.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Build
layers.description: Build configuration, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and build scripts (SConstructs, Makefiles).

layers.name: Testing & Verification
layers.description: Unit tests, safety tests, integration tests, and test infrastructure.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation, README, and reference materials.

layers.name: Examples & Utilities
layers.description: Example code, utility scripts, and development tools.

layers.name: Utilities & Miscellaneous
layers.description: Additional utilities and miscellaneous project files.

tour.title: Welcome to Panda
tour.description: Start with the project README to understand panda's role as a safety-critical CAN adapter for openpilot vehicle control. Panda speaks CAN/CAN FD on the STM32H725 microcontroller.

tour.title: Board Hardware Fundamentals
tour.description: Explore the board layer — hardware initialization and core STM32 definitions. Start with board.h to see the hardware abstractions.

tour.title: CAN Bus Implementation
tour.description: Understand the CAN driver architecture. The panda firmware handles both CAN and CAN FD communication for vehicle networks.

tour.title: Safety Layer
tour.description: Examine the safety-critical layer. This enforces vehicle-specific safety models from opendbc to protect against unsafe control commands.

tour.title: Python Userspace Library
tour.description: Learn how the Python userspace library interfaces with the panda firmware. This is how openpilot communicates with the hardware.

tour.title: Build System
tour.description: Understand the build infrastructure. Panda uses SCons for C firmware builds and Python packaging for the userspace library.

tour.title: Testing & Verification
tour.description: Review the comprehensive test suite. Safety testing includes unit tests for each vehicle variant and MISRA C:2012 compliance checks.

tour.title: Example Usage
tour.description: See practical examples of using panda in a vehicle. These examples show how to send and receive CAN messages.

tour.title: Development Workflow
tour.description: Explore the development tools, Docker environment, and CI infrastructure used to develop and test panda.
